The Woman King (2022)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $67,328,130Details
International Box Office $26,944,504
Worldwide Box Office $94,272,634
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,844,157
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,400,449
Total $7,244,606
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The story of the Agojie, the all-female unit of warriors who protected the African Kingdom of Dahomey in the 1800s with skills and a fierceness unlike anything the world has ever seen. General Nanisca trains the next generation of recruits and readies them for battle against an enemy determined to destroy their way of life. Some things are worth fighting for…

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$19,051,442 (28.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.53 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:71.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$50,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,765 opening theaters/3,765 max. theaters, 6.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $76,465,088

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 16th, 2022 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: September 15th, 2022 (Wide) (Nigeria)
September 22nd, 2022 (Wide), released as A Mulher Rei (Brazil)
September 28th, 2022 (Wide) (France)
October 6th, 2022 (Wide), released as Der Frauenkönig (Germany)
October 6th, 2022 (Wide) (Portugal)
Video Release: November 22nd, 2022 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of strong violence, some disturbing material, thematic content, brief language and partial nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2747 (cert #53622), 8/31/2022)
Running Time: 135 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: F-Rated, F-Rated, Triple
Geographical setting: Africa
Plot point: Rescue, Sex Crimes, Unexpected Families
Social setting: Slavery
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 1700s, 1800s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Welle Entertainment, Jack Blue Productions, Juvee, Tri-Star Pictures, Entertainment One, TSG Entertainment
Production Countries: United States, Canada
Languages: English

Synopsis

The story of the Agojie, the all-female unit of warriors who protected the African Kingdom of Dahomey in the 1800s with skills and a fierceness unlike anything the world has ever seen. General Nanisca trains the next generation of recruits and readies them for battle against an enemy determined to destroy their way of life. Some things are worth fighting for…
